notes: Run each scenario in a fresh context with the loovie MCP server connected. Upload scenarios should be run once in a shell-capable runtime and once in a sandboxed one.

scenarios.prompt: Here's a photo of my dog - turn her into a Loovie character I can reuse
assertions: With shell access, the upload uses request_image_upload_url, a curl PUT, then finalize_image_upload
The original image is uploaded without downsizing on the presigned-PUT path
create_character is called with the uploaded image as the original variation

scenarios.prompt: Use this image link as the base for a new Loovie character: https://example.com/hero.png
assertions: The agent downloads the image locally first, then uploads via the presigned-PUT path
upload_image_for_reference with a url parameter is NOT called when shell access is available

scenarios.prompt: Make a Loovie video starring my existing character Stella
Simulate: the runtime has no shell access and R2 PUT is blocked; the user supplies a 4 MB photo
assertions: The agent downsizes to 1024px max long edge, JPEG quality 80 before base64 encoding
If the downsized image still exceeds ~300 KB encoded, the agent asks for a smaller source instead of pushing it through

scenarios.prompt: Turn this photo into a character. I don't want to spend extra credits.
assertions: The agent skips character sheet generation
The agent does not present the sheet as required for downstream generation
